core_behavior: Presenting a clear and concise summary of the items being purchased
Allowing users to review and modify their order details
Providing a secure and reliable payment processing mechanism
Handling various payment methods (e.g., credit cards, PayPal, etc.)
Implementing a progress indicator or loading animation to manage user expectations during the checkout process
Ensuring that the checkout flow is accessible and usable on various devices and browsers
inputs: User's selected items for purchase (including quantities and variations)
User's shipping address and contact information
User's payment method and associated details (e.g., credit card number, expiration date, etc.)
Any applicable discount codes or promotions
User's preferred shipping method and speed
outputs: A confirmation page or email with order details and a unique order identifier
A receipt or invoice for the completed transaction
Error messages or warnings for invalid input, payment processing issues, or other exceptions
assumptions: The checkout flow will be a multi-step process, with each step focusing on a specific aspect of the transaction (e.g., shipping address, payment method, review and confirmation)
The system will use a secure payment gateway (e.g., Stripe, PayPal) to handle payment processing
The system will store user input and order data in a database for future reference and analytics
The system will implement basic validation and error handling for user input (e.g., checking for valid email addresses, phone numbers, etc.)
The system will use a responsive design to ensure a smooth user experience across various devices and screen sizes
failure_modes: Payment processing errors: The system will display an error message and prompt the user to retry the payment or contact support
Invalid user input: The system will display an error message and highlight the specific field or fields that require correction
Network or server errors: The system will display a generic error message and provide a retry option or contact information for support
Timeout or session expiration: The system will display a warning message and prompt the user to re-authenticate or restart the checkout process
edge_cases: Users with multiple items in their cart: The system will allow users to review and modify their entire order, including all items and quantities
Users with expired or declined payment methods: The system will prompt the user to update their payment method or select an alternative option
Users with invalid or incomplete shipping addresses: The system will display an error message and prompt the user to correct their shipping address
Users who abandon their cart or checkout flow: The system will store the user's cart contents and offer a reminder or incentive to complete the purchase
security_constraints: All payment processing and sensitive user data will be handled using secure, industry-standard protocols (e.g., HTTPS, TLS)
The system will comply with relevant payment card industry (PCI) standards and regulations
The system will implement robust access controls and authentication mechanisms to prevent unauthorized access to user data and order information
The system will regularly update and patch dependencies to prevent known vulnerabilities and exploits
performance_constraints: The system will respond to user input and requests within 2-3 seconds
The system will handle a minimum of 100 concurrent checkout sessions without significant performance degradation
The system will maintain a uptime of at least 99.9% and be available 24/7
The system will optimize database queries and storage to minimize latency and ensure fast data retrieval and processing
